conditions.critical_condition_basis: elution volume independent of molecular weight
conditions.evidence_text: Using the non-polar C18 stationary phase, the critical composition of the MeOH–water eluent, first established for PEG standards (Mw = 200 to 1000 g mol-1) using 1 mM LiCl concentration, was determined at 88.8:11.2 (%, v/v) and allows all PEG chains to have the same elution volume (Ve = 2.33 mL), independently of their molecular mass.
conditions.notes: Critical conditions established with LiCl. The text notes that changing the cation (e.g., to Cs+) shifts the elution mode to interaction, meaning critical conditions were not achieved with other salts on this column.
conditions.physical_address: Universites Aix-Marseille I, II et III – CNRS, UMR 6264: Laboratoire Chimie Provence, Spectrometries Appliquees a la Chimie Structurale, F-13397 Marseille, France

conditions.critical_condition_basis: elution volume independent of molecular weight
conditions.evidence_text: For the selected system, critical conditions were obtained with a 93.5:6.5 (%, v/v) MeOH–water eluent, as indicated by the same elution volume (Ve = 4.15 mL) measured for all PEG chains.
conditions.notes: Critical conditions established with LiCl on polar stationary phase. The text notes that changing the cation (e.g., to Cs+) shifts the elution mode to exclusion, meaning critical conditions were not achieved with other salts on this column.
conditions.physical_address: Universites Aix-Marseille I, II et III – CNRS, UMR 6264: Laboratoire Chimie Provence, Spectrometries Appliquees a la Chimie Structurale, F-13397 Marseille, France
